about:
Linear is a software company that builds the issue tracking and project management tool of the same name, designed for high-performance product teams. The product is known for its keyboard-driven interface, snappy local-first architecture, and opinionated workflows that nudge teams toward shipping smaller changes more often.

The company was founded in 2019 by former engineers and designers from companies like Coinbase, Airbnb, and Uber. It operates as a fully distributed team across North America and Europe, with a strong culture of writing, async collaboration, and craft. Linear is profitable and well-capitalized, having raised funding from investors including Sequoia and Accel.

The team is small relative to the impact of the product, and each engineer is expected to own significant pieces of the system end-to-end. Frontend work happens in TypeScript and React on top of a custom sync engine, and the company values engineers who care deeply about UI polish, responsiveness, and developer experience.

Vercel is the company behind Next.js and the eponymous frontend cloud platform, providing hosting, edge functions, and a developer workflow built around git-based deployments. Its mission is to enable developers to build performant, dynamic web applications without having to think about infrastructure.

The company is headquartered in San Francisco but is heavily remote, with team members across the US, Europe, and beyond. Engineering work spans the framework layer (Next.js, Turbopack), the platform itself, and adjacent open-source projects. The company places a strong emphasis on shipping fast and iterating in public.

Figma is a browser-based collaborative design platform used by product teams at companies of every size. Its core innovation is a multiplayer editor that allows designers and engineers to work together in the same document in real time, eliminating the file-sync and version-control friction of legacy desktop design tools.

The company was founded in 2012 and has grown into one of the dominant design tools in the industry. Engineering culture is known for its rigor around performance, with deep work going into the WebAssembly-based rendering engine that powers the canvas.

Notion builds an all-in-one workspace that combines notes, documents, databases, and project management into a single tool. The product is highly customizable and has been adopted by both individuals and large enterprises as a flexible knowledge management platform.

The company is headquartered in San Francisco and has a growing international presence, including major offices in Dublin and Tokyo. Engineering challenges span from real-time sync of structured data to building a complex block-based editor that scales to millions of users.

Anthropic is an AI safety company focused on building reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems. The company is best known for the Claude family of large language models and for foundational research on AI alignment, mechanistic interpretability, and Constitutional AI.

Founded in 2021, the company has rapidly grown into one of the leading AI labs, with research and engineering teams working on everything from model training infrastructure to applied product surfaces such as Claude.ai and the Claude API. The culture is research-forward, mission-driven, and deeply technical.
